sources.issuing_organisation: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence
sources.population: Children, young people, and adults with psoriasis, including skin, nail, joint, severity, impact, and referral qualifiers.
sources.clinical_setting: Primary and specialist assessment and management of psoriasis.
sources.applicability_note: Exact for psoriasis identity, distribution, extent, nail involvement, high-impact sites, symptom and life impact, systemic upset, and joint-symptom assessment. Treatment and referral decisions are not mapped, and UAE adaptation remains required.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.2.1.1–1.2.1.12 — assessment, severity, sites, and impact
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documenting psoriasis type, duration, extent, distribution, nails, high-impact sites, symptoms, systemic upset, daily-life impact, and wellbeing without inferring an individual diagnosis or treatment.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.2.2.1–1.2.2.4 — psoriatic arthritis assessment and referral qualifiers
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports asking about joint symptoms and recording clinician-led specialist assessment or referral only when completed or arranged.
